title: Passive voice: present and past simple
lesson: In the passive, the object of the action becomes the subject. Form it with the verb 'be' + past participle: present simple (am/is/are + participle) or past simple (was/were + participle). Use the passive when the action matters more than who does it. If you mention the doer, introduce it with 'by': 'The window was broken by the boys.'

items.explanation: Present simple passive: is/are + past participle. 'English' is singular, so 'is spoken' (speak → spoken).
Present simple passive with a plural subject: 'are' + past participle. make → made.
Past simple passive: was/were + past participle. Singular subject 'bridge' takes 'was'; build → built.
Past simple passive with a plural subject: 'were' + past participle. send → sent.
A regular action in the present uses the present simple passive: is + cleaned (regular -ed participle).

blanks.hint: past simple passive with an agent

items.explanation: Past simple passive: 'was' + caught (catch → caught). The doer is introduced with 'by': 'by a neighbour'.
Present simple passive with a plural subject 'millions': 'are' + sent. send → sent.

items.text: The painting {1} by a famous artist in 1889.

blanks.hint: past simple passive with an agent

items.explanation: Past simple passive: 'was' + painted. The agent follows 'by': 'by a famous artist'.
'Rice' is uncountable and treated as singular, so 'is grown' (grow → grown).

items.text: The windows {1} during the storm last night.
items.explanation: Past simple passive with a plural subject 'windows': 'were' + broken. break → broken.

items.text: This song {1} by people of all ages.

blanks.hint: present simple passive with an agent

items.explanation: Present simple passive: 'is' + loved. The people who love it are introduced with 'by'.

items.text: The results {1} to the students last week.
items.explanation: Past simple passive with a plural subject 'results': 'were' + explained (regular -ed participle).
Modal passive: modal + 'be' + past participle. 'must be completed' expresses obligation (complete → completed).

items.text: From the top of the hill, the whole city {1}.
items.explanation: Modal passive: 'can' + 'be' + past participle expresses possibility. see → seen.

items.text: The road is closed because it {1} at the moment.

blanks.hint: present continuous passive: is/are + being + participle

items.explanation: Present continuous passive: 'is/are' + 'being' + past participle for an action happening now. repair → repaired.

blanks.hint: present perfect passive: has/have + been + participle

items.explanation: Present perfect passive: 'has/have' + 'been' + past participle. Singular subject 'work' takes 'has'; finish → finished.

items.text: All the tickets {1} by the fans within minutes.

blanks.hint: present perfect passive with an agent

items.explanation: Present perfect passive: 'have' + 'been' + past participle with the plural subject 'tickets'. The agent follows 'by'; sell → sold.
